articles.title: Aaron Rodgers’ future grows more intriguing as Mike Tomlin fuels fresh debate by backing veteran QB
articles.description: Mike Tomlin’s early NBC appearance delivered a strong take on Aaron Rodgers’ future, predicting the quarterback will continue playing in 2026. Drawing from a year of close interaction, Tomlin highlighted Rodgers’ deep attachment to the daily process of football. However, uncertainty remains around Rodgers’ offseason approach and timing. With limited options across the league and a crowded Steelers quarterback room, Pittsburgh still appears the most likely destination if Rodgers decides to return.

articles.title: IPL 2026: 'Nandri Chennai' - Shubman Gill shares special post with yellow whistle after GT beat CSK
articles.description: Shubman Gill celebrated Gujarat Titans’ win over Chennai Super Kings with a “Nandri Chennai” post. GT chased 159 comfortably, winning by eight wickets with 20 balls to spare. Sai Sudharsan starred with 87, supported by Gill and Jos Buttler. Earlier, Ruturaj Gaikwad made 74*, but Kagiso Rabada’s strikes restricted CSK.

articles.title: Tomlin: Exit partly rooted in recent playoff woes
articles.description: Mike Tomlin told NBC's Maria Taylor that he walked away, in part, because of the recent lack of playoff success and a belief that some of his veterans were "worthy of the excitement and the optimism associated with new leadership."

articles.title: LSG vs KKR Live Score, IPL 2026: Both Teams Eye To Keep Playoffs Chances Alive
articles.description: Lucknow Super Giants vs Kolkata Knight Riders Live Score, IPL 2026 T20, Match 38: Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) are facing the Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) in match 38 of the IPL 2026 at Ekana Cricket Stadium. After losing four straight games, LSG is under a lot of pressure going into this match. They did well to limit the Rajasthan Royals previously, but in their most recent match, they failed in a pursuit of 160. With a half-century, Mitchell Marsh at last found form, although the batting lineup has been inconsistent this season. With three losses in their first three games in Lucknow, their home record has also been a big worry. On the other hand, KKR enters the match fresh off a week-long holiday and their season-opening victory over Rajasthan Royals. Rinku Singh made a strong comeback with a half-century, while Varun Chakravarthy shone with an outstanding 3 for 14 and was named Player of the Match. With indications that Matheesha Pathirana may be available for selection, the squad will try to build momentum from this victory. Squads Lucknow Super Giants: Mitchell Marsh, Ayush Badoni, Rishabh Pant(w/c), Aiden Markram, Nicholas Pooran, Himmat Singh, Mukul Choudhary, Mohammed Shami, Mayank Yadav, Digvesh Singh Rathi, Mohsin Khan, Prince Yadav, George Linde, Manimaran Siddharth, Matthew Breetzke, Abdul Samad, Avesh Khan, Josh Inglis, Anrich Nortje, Arjun Tendulkar, Shahbaz Ahmed, Akash Maharaj Singh, Akshat Raghuwanshi, Arshin Kulkarni, Naman Tiwari Kolkata Knight Riders: Tim Seifert(w), Ajinkya Rahane(c), Angkrish Raghuvanshi, Cameron Green, Rovman Powell, Rinku Singh, Ramandeep Singh, Anukul Roy, Sunil Narine, Vaibhav Arora, Kartik Tyagi, Varun Chakaravarthy, Manish Pandey, Finn Allen, Tejasvi Dahiya, Navdeep Saini, Rahul Tripathi, Sarthak Ranjan, Rachin Ravindra, Prashant Solanki, Blessing Muzarabani, Saurabh Dubey, Matheesha Pathirana, Umran Malik, Daksh Kamra

articles.title: Zhao Xintong holds off Ding Junhui in tense battle to reach Crucible quarters
articles.description: Defending champion faces Shaun Murphy after 13-9 win Mark Selby and Yu Wize level at 4-4 after first session Zhao Xintong moved closer to cracking the so-called “Crucible Curse” as he booked his quarter-final place at the World Snooker Championship, wrapping up a hard-fought 13-9 win over compatriot Ding Junhui. The defending champion shrugged off some evident nerves to build on a 9-7 overnight advantage despite losing an error-strewn, 46-minute opener that saw Ding temporarily reduce the arrears to a single frame.
